payload_model_report.purpose: Expose how the simulation models realistic IIoT event size, protocol/security overhead, compute demand, DRL decision metadata, and cloud/API monitoring export size.

formulas.event_payload_kb: sum of all payload component fields
formulas.task_cpu_cycles: task_size_kb * severity CPU cycles per KB
formulas.monitoring_export_kb: cloud/API record overhead by record type

payload_components.label: Event metadata
payload_components.counts: sensor ID, edge gateway, timestamp, severity, priority, deadline, and event reason

payload_components.label: Sensor history window
payload_components.counts: recent readings from the same sensor used to detect spikes, trends, and volatility

payload_components.label: Fault/spike window
payload_components.counts: extra same-sensor values around an abnormal spike or fault window

payload_components.label: Diagnostic logs
payload_components.counts: diagnostic context attached when the event needs explanation

payload_components.label: Machine context
payload_components.counts: equipment, zone, edge gateway, operating/source context currently modeled from topology metadata

payload_components.label: Calculated features
payload_components.counts: derived values such as peaks, averages, RMS, threshold deviations, and trend features

payload_components.label: Device/security metadata
payload_components.counts: device identity and security/authentication metadata estimate

compute_stages.label: Intake validation
compute_stages.counts: message parsing, timestamp/device check, missing-value and range validation

compute_stages.label: Threshold classification
compute_stages.counts: compare the current sensor reading against normal/warning/critical thresholds

compute_stages.label: Feature extraction
compute_stages.counts: derive values such as peaks, averages, RMS, deviation, and trend features

compute_stages.label: History analysis
compute_stages.counts: same-sensor spike, trend, and volatility checks using recent readings

compute_stages.label: Aggregation
compute_stages.counts: combine events, edge summaries, or regional context

compute_stages.label: Decision packaging
compute_stages.counts: attach DRL route, factors, deadline result, and monitoring metadata

implemented_algorithms.description: Checks event identity, timestamp, missing readings, and configured reading ranges.
Classifies the current reading from the event sensor type against normal/warning/critical thresholds and derives event severity.
Calculates same-sensor historical baseline, delta, spike, trend, volatility, and severity-confidence features.
Detects same-sensor spike, rising/falling trend, and volatility patterns from recent readings.
Summarizes recent event windows for cloud warning/normal/periodic reports.
Combines severity, feature score, history anomaly, and deadline result into a cloud risk score.

literature_alignment.mapping: Modeled input data transferred by the IIoT task: event payload plus protocol/security overhead.
Processing burden derived from task size and severity, then split into validation, classification, feature extraction, history analysis, aggregation, cloud analytics, and decision packaging.
Severity defines the timing requirement; DRL rewards successful decisions that satisfy the deadline.
DRL chooses where the task should be processed using local edge, edge-to-edge, edge-to-fog, fog-to-fog, or cloud escalation actions.
Assigned tasks reserve destination compute capacity and route link traffic for a simulated duration, influencing later decisions.
Routing evaluates static link properties together with active simulated traffic to estimate network pressure.

literature_reference_examples.paper: Distributed task offloading in edge computing: A multi-objective adaptive deep reinforcement learning algorithm
literature_reference_examples.supports: Multi-objective DRL task offloading with adaptive decisions.
literature_reference_examples.implemented_mapping: DQN route selection using latency, congestion, energy, task size, bandwidth, compute pressure, reliability, and deadline.

literature_reference_examples.paper: Energy-efficient task offloading in the Industrial Internet of Things: A Lyapunov-guided multi-agent deep reinforcement learning approach
literature_reference_examples.supports: IIoT task offloading, energy-aware decisions, and resource-aware DRL framing.
literature_reference_examples.implemented_mapping: Energy cost remains a DRL factor, while dynamic node/link load models changing resource state.

literature_reference_examples.paper: An advanced deep reinforcement learning algorithm for three-layer D2D-edge-cloud computing architecture for efficient task offloading in the Internet of Things
literature_reference_examples.supports: Three-layer device/edge/cloud offloading and DQN-style task routing.
literature_reference_examples.implemented_mapping: Sensor/edge/fog/cloud topology with local edge, edge-to-edge, edge-to-fog, fog-to-fog, and cloud escalation actions.

literature_reference_examples.paper: Multi-objective task offloading optimization using deep reinforcement learning with resource distribution clustering
literature_reference_examples.supports: Multi-objective DRL offloading with resource distribution awareness.
literature_reference_examples.implemented_mapping: Candidate routing considers nearby resources and now reacts to dynamic compute/link usage.

literature_reference_examples.paper: Deep reinforcement learning for optimizing computation latency in wireless-powered Multi-Access Edge Computing systems: A partial offloading approach
literature_reference_examples.supports: Computation latency minimization, offloading decisions, and resource management.
literature_reference_examples.implemented_mapping: Task CPU cycles and compute demand ratio are modeled separately from task/network size.

literature_reference_examples.paper: Reliable and efficient computation offloading for dependency-aware tasks in IIoT using evolutionary multi-objective optimization
literature_reference_examples.supports: IIoT computation offloading with reliability and multi-objective optimization.
literature_reference_examples.implemented_mapping: Reliability risk is retained as a DRL factor and deadline success is reported.

literature_reference_examples.paper: Dynamic offloading strategy for computational energy efficiency of wireless power transfer based MEC networks in industry 5.0
literature_reference_examples.supports: Dynamic offloading, task computational model, local/edge computational model, and resource allocation.
literature_reference_examples.implemented_mapping: Dynamic node load and task_cpu_cycles represent changing compute demand without forcing fixed layer-specific algorithm roles.

dynamic_resource_allocation.purpose: Show that resource state is updated after each offloading decision, so later DRL decisions see changed node load and link traffic.
